2606.06240.title: TOKI: A Bitemporal Operator Algebra for Contradiction Resolution in LLM-Agent Persistent Memory
2606.06240.paper_summary: Persistent memory for an LLM agent is a write-heavy substrate: every belief update is a versioned write, and a new claim may contradict a stored one. Production systems use four resolution heuristics (last-writer-wins, evidence-weighted merge, await-confirmation, per-rule policy), yet none declares the isolation level it assumes or the write-time anomalies it admits. We show that contradiction resolution is write-time concurrency control and make the missing contract explicit. TOKI types the four heuristics as one family of bitemporal operators over a dual-row schema, each with an isolation precondition and a provenance annotation that preserves the losing fact in an audit row. Four soundness theorems close the contract across isolation, schema, and provenance, lift the guarantees to operator pipelines, and extend the fold operators to n-ary conflict sets. A tightness companion proves that, within the relational schedule model, keyed logging of the adjudicating judge is necessary for replay consistency, which every audited baseline omits. A verdict matrix over eight systems localizes the gap: every baseline that keeps a language-model judge on the write path admits at least one of three write-time anomalies (replay inconsistency, belief-drift skew, audit erasure); a content-addressed engine-layer comparator avoids them only by removing the judge, and TOKI alone excludes all three while keeping it. On its one natural-workload slice the audit-row defence moves LoCoMo by 0.86, and ablating the typed memory layer removes 0.49 accuracy on 1,444 answerable LoCoMo questions; the cross-system comparison stays underpowered and claims no superiority. The contribution is the contract: a write-time correctness specification, proved sound across isolation, schema, and provenance, pinning the guarantee every production heuristic assumes but no deployed system makes explicit.
2606.06240.paper_summary_zh: 持久記憶對於 LLM 代理來說是一個寫入密集的基底：每次信念更新都是一個版本化的寫入，而新的主張可能會與已存儲的主張相矛盾。生產系統使用四種解決啟發式（最後寫入者獲勝、證據加權合併、等待確認、每條規則政策），但沒有一個聲明其假設的隔離級別或承認的寫入時間異常。我們展示了矛盾解決是寫入時間的併發控制，並使缺失的合約變得明確。TOKI 將這四種啟發式類型化為一個雙時態運算符的家族，基於雙行模式，每個運算符都有一個隔離前置條件和一個保留失敗事實的來源註釋，該註釋位於審計行中。四個健全性定理在隔離、模式和來源之間關閉合約，將保證提升到運算符管道，並將折疊運算符擴展到 n-元衝突集。一個緊密性伴隨證明，在關係調度模型中，裁決法官的鍵控日誌對於重放一致性是必要的，而每個經審計的基線都忽略了這一點。對八個系統的判決矩陣定位了差距：每個在寫入路徑上保持語言模型法官的基線都承認至少一種三種寫入時間異常（重放不一致、信念漂移偏差、審計抹除）；一個內容地址引擎層比較器僅通過移除法官來避免它們，而 TOKI 獨自排除所有三種同時保留法官。在其一個自然工作負載切片中，審計行防禦將 LoCoMo 移動了 0.86，並且去除類型化記憶層在 1,444 個可回答的 LoCoMo 問題上減少了 0.49 的準確性；跨系統比較仍然能力不足，並聲稱沒有優越性。貢獻是合約：一個寫入時間正確性規範，證明在隔離、模式和來源之間是健全的，釘住了每個生產啟發式所假設的保證，但沒有任何已部署系統明確表達。
2606.06240.author: Ziming Wang et.al.
